content.text: The movement of goods and services from supply locations to demand locations necessitates the need for transport. The pace of development of a country depends upon the production of goods and services as well as their movement over space. Efficient means of transport are prerequisites for fast development.

content.title: Interdependence
content.text: Transport, communication, and trade are complementary to each other. Today, the world has been converted into a large village with the help of efficient and fast-moving transport and a well-developed communication system.

content.title: Why Roadways are preferred:
items: Lower construction cost compared to railway lines.
Can traverse dissected and undulating topography.
Can negotiate higher gradients of slopes (e.g., Himalayas).
Economical for few persons and smaller amount of goods over short distances.
Provides door-to-door service, reducing loading/unloading costs.
Acts as a feeder to other modes like railway stations, air and sea ports.

rows: Links Delhi-Kolkata-Chennai-Mumbai by 6-lane highways. Reduces time/distance.
Connects extreme parts of the country; primary road systems.
Links state capital with district headquarters.
Connects district headquarters with other places in the district.
Links villages with towns under Pradhan Mantri Grameen Sadak Yojana.

content.title: Atal Tunnel
content.text: The World’s longest Highway tunnel (9.02 Km) built by BRO. It connects Manali to Lahaul-Spiti valley throughout the year, bypassing heavy snowfall areas in the Pir Panjal range.

Railways are the principal mode of transportation for freight and passengers in India. They bind the economic life of the country and accelerate the development of industry and agriculture. The first train ran from Mumbai to Thane in 1853 (34 km).

content.title: Factors influencing Railway network:
items: Physiographic: Northern plains (favourable), Himalayas (unfavourable relief).
Economic: High population density and agricultural resources in plains vs sparse population in mountains.
Administrative: Sandy plains of Rajasthan and swamps of Gujarat pose obstacles for layout.

content.title: Konkan Railway
content.text: Developed along the west coast, it facilitates movement in a vital economic region but faces challenges like landslides and sinking of tracks.
Originally used for water, pipelines now transport crude oil, petroleum products, and natural gas to refineries and fertilizer plants. Solids can also be transported as slurry.

content.title: Advantages and Networks:
items: Minimal running costs after high initial setup.
Rules out trans-shipment losses or delays.
Major Networks: (1) Upper Assam to Kanpur, (2) Salaya (Gujarat) to Jalandhar (Punjab), (3) HVJ (Hazira-Vijaipur-Jagdishpur) gas pipeline.

content.text: Waterways are most suitable for carrying heavy and bulky goods. India has 14,500 km of inland navigation waterways. The National Waterways Act, 2016 declared 111 inland waterways as National Waterways.

content.title: National Waterways (NW):
items: NW 1: Ganga river between Prayagraj and Haldia (1620 km).
NW 2: Brahmaputra river between Sadiya and Dhubri (891 km).
NW 3: West-Coast Canal in Kerala (205 km).
NW 4: Godavari and Krishna rivers (1078 km).
NW 5: River Brahmani and Matai river channels (588 km).

content.text: India has a 7,516.6 km coastline with 12 major ports handling 95% of foreign trade.

rows: Tidal port; handles granary/industrial belt exports.
Biggest port; spacious natural harbour. Includes JNPT.
Caters to iron ore export from Kudremukh mines.
Extreme south-western port; natural harbour in a lagoon.
South-eastern port with a natural harbour.
One of the oldest artificial ports; ranked 2nd in volume.
Inland riverine port; requires constant dredging of Hoogly.
Developed as a subsidiary to relieve pressure on Kolkata.

content.text: Air travel is the most comfortable and prestigious mode, essential for covering difficult terrains like high mountains or dense forests. It is vital for the North-eastern part of India due to rivers and dissected relief.

content.title: Special Services
content.text: Pawanhans Helicopters Ltd. provides services to ONGC and difficult terrains. UDAN (Ude Desh ka Aam Nagrik) promotes regional connectivity through affordable flights.

India has the largest postal network in the world. First-class mail (cards/envelopes) is airlifted, while second-class mail (packets/newspapers) is surface-carried. Six mail channels (Rajdhani, Metro, etc.) facilitate quick delivery.

content.title: Digital India
content.text: Focuses on transformer India into a knowledge-based economy. Telecom networks cover villages with 24-hour STD facilities through space and communication tech integration.

content.title: Major Means:
items: Radio: Akashwani broadcasts in national/regional languages.
Television: Doordarshan is one of the largest terrestrial networks.
Press: Newspapers in 100 languages/dialects (Hindi is largest).
Films: India is the largest producer of feature films worldwide.

content.text: Trade between countries is international trade, considered the 'economic barometer'. Components include Exports and Imports. Advancement in international trade is an index of economic prosperity.

content.title: Balance of Trade
content.text: Favourable: Value of Exports > Value of Imports. Unfavourable: Value of Imports > Value of Exports.
Tourism promotes national integration, supports local handicrafts, and earns foreign exchange. It includes heritage, eco, adventure, medical, and business tourism.

content.title: Schemes
content.text: Swadesh Darshan 2.0, PRASHAD, and Paryatan Mitra are active government initiatives to boost this industry.

glossary.definition: The essential systems of transport and communication that support a nation's economy and social integration.
An ambitious road development project linking the major mega cities of India: Delhi, Kolkata, Chennai, and Mumbai.
Specified river or canal stretches declared by the government as major routes for inland navigation.
The inland area served by a port or trading center, providing goods for export and consuming imports.
The difference between the value of a country's total exports and its total imports.
Ude Desh ka Aam Nagrik; a regional connectivity scheme to make flying affordable for the common citizen.

summary_points: Efficient transport and communication networks are the prerequisites for fast economic development and global trade links.
Indias road network is one of the largest in the world, classified into six classes including Super Highways and National Highways.
Railways remain the principal mode for long-distance transport, though they face challenges like track maintenance and public misuse.
Pipelines have revolutionized the transport of oil and gas, reducing trans-shipment delays and running costs significantly.
The vast coastline and numerous inland waterways provide economical and eco-friendly modes for bulk trade through major ports.
Modern communication systems, from the vast postal network to high-tech digital connectivity, bridge the gap between rural and urban India.
International trade and tourism serve as critical economic barometers, reflecting India's growing influence in the global marketplace.
